top_comments.text:
Here the actual project, saved you a click:

https://valent-in.github.io/pulseq/

FWIW, the traditional, 1980s approach uses fingerprints, which are binary vectors. Here's the same search using RDKit's "Morgan" fingerprints, with radius 2, and count enumeration simulation enabled, using my "chemfp" project.

  % curl -O 'https://gist.githubusercontent.com/fzliu/8052bd4d609bc6260ab7e8c838d2f518/raw/f1c9efb816d6b8514c0a643323f7afa29372b1c4/fda_approved_structures.csv'
  % chemfp csv2fps --typ

> fake mouse

That's what I'm doing with my new project, Valet. It's a Raspberry Pi configured to be a fake mouse, keyboard, and an Android (touch stylus). Works well on iOS and Android.

For code I usually use

~/Code

  ~/projects # for projects that span multiple languages

  ~/courses # For the various programming courses

  ~/valet # PHP projects using Valet+

  ~/devilbox # For Dockerized

  ~/elixir

  ~/node

  etc...
For notes I use Inkdrop and split things by hobbies/projects.

One thing I've been having trouble organising is photos. More specifically, organising original and retouched versions. I&

It's my lovely city of San Francisco. The list of things the locals have been grumpy about is quite large. Let me list some of the ones I know of:

1. An ice-cream store opening on a block with another ice-cream store on it. Owner eventually gave up, but there's an entertaining live tweet thread about it https://twitter.com/_fruchtose/status&#

npm_packages.name: electron-to-chromium
npm_packages.description: Provides a list of electron-to-chromium version mappings

npm_packages.name: expo-constants
npm_packages.description: Provides system information that remains constant throughout the lifetime of your app.

npm_packages.name: expo-asset
npm_packages.description: An Expo universal module to download assets and pass them into other APIs

npm_packages.name: expo-keep-awake
npm_packages.description: Provides a React component that prevents the screen sleeping when rendered. It also exposes static methods to control the behavior imperatively.

contrarian_analysis.bull_case_flaw: Valent touts advanced AI for narrative and bot detection, but provides no technical transparency or evidence of proprietary models or breakthrough capabilities—everything described could be built with off-the-shelf LLMs and basic social listening APIs.
contrarian_analysis.elephant_in_room: How does Valent differentiate from dozens of existing media monitoring and disinformation detection platforms, and what prevents a comms team from just using cheaper, better-known tools from established vendors?

timing_assessment.reasoning: Disinformation is a real and growing problem, but the market is crowded and buyers are skeptical; Valent's timing is neither obviously too early nor too late, but their lack of technical depth and differentiation means they risk being ignored unless they prove unique value.

incumbent_threat.most_dangerous_competitor: Microsoft (via Azure Cognitive Services), Google (Jigsaw, Cloud AI), OpenAI (with a dedicated enterprise offering)
incumbent_threat.killer_feature: A bundled 'AI Narrative & Bot Detection' dashboard integrated into Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace, leveraging their massive data access and existing enterprise relationships, offered at a fraction of Valent’s price.
incumbent_threat.time_to_threat: 3-6 months if incumbents prioritize this vertical

hidden_assumptions: Comms teams will trust a startup with sensitive crisis and reputation data
AI can reliably detect and predict narrative and bot activity better than human analysts
Clients will pay a premium for an all-in-one dashboard versus piecemeal solutions
Regulatory and privacy concerns won't block adoption

moat_reality.claimed_moat: Unified, agentic AI platform for comms teams with predictive, context-aware analytics and reporting
moat_reality.actual_moat: Minimal—features are easily replicable, and no evidence of proprietary data, algorithms, or network effects; brand and client list are their only partial moat.

contrarian_analysis.honest_take: Valent's pitch is long on buzzwords and case studies, but short on technical substance and defensibility. Unless they reveal real IP or unique data, they're just another dashboard riding the AI hype cycle.

primary_trigger.trigger: Surge in AI-driven disinformation campaigns targeting elections and geopolitical events
primary_trigger.evidence: High-profile incidents of AI-generated fake news and deepfakes influencing public opinion have been widely reported since late 2023; governments and organizations are urgently seeking solutions.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Advances in large language models and generative AI make online manipulation more scalable and harder to detect
secondary_triggers.relevance: Valent Projects' AI-powered media analysis tools are now essential to keep pace with increasingly sophisticated threats.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Proposed and enacted regulations (e.g., EU Digital Services Act, UK Online Safety Bill) require platforms and organizations to monitor and report disinformation
secondary_triggers.relevance: Creates compliance pressure and new budget lines for disinformation tracking solutions.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Competitors focus on enterprise and government clients but lack real-time, multi-lingual tracking capabilities
secondary_triggers.relevance: Valent Projects can differentiate by offering more agile, comprehensive solutions.

secondary_triggers.trigger: News consumers and organizations increasingly rely on social media and alternative channels, accelerating the spread of misinformation
secondary_triggers.relevance: The need for automated, scalable monitoring is greater than ever.

why_now.why_not_earlier: Two years ago, generative AI tools were less advanced and less accessible, making large-scale, convincing disinformation campaigns rarer and easier to detect; regulatory and market urgency were also lower.
why_now.why_not_later: Delaying risks ceding market share to incumbents and missing the window as organizations scramble to comply with new regulations and respond to escalating threats.
why_now.newsletter_hook: AI-powered disinformation is surging—Valent Projects is launching now to help organizations fight back before the threat gets out of control.

takeaways.title: Adopt Multi-Agent Modular Architectures
takeaways.insight: Valent’s Ariadne platform leverages agentic, modular architectures for narrative and bot detection, allowing for separation of concerns and parallel development. This enables rapid iteration and targeted improvements to individual agents (e.g., narrative detection, bot identification).
takeaways.how_to_apply: Design your system as a set of loosely coupled agents, each responsible for a specific analytic task. Use message queues (e.g., Kafka, RabbitMQ) or event-driven frameworks to coordinate agent outputs.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your use case is simple or has tight latency requirements where inter-agent communication overhead outweighs modularity benefits.
takeaways.example:
Each agent runs as a microservice:

# Example agent registration (Python pseudo-code)
register_agent('bot_detector', BotDetectionAgent())
register_agent('narrative_detector', NarrativeDetectionAgent())

takeaways.title: Use Micro-Model Meshes for Cost Control
takeaways.insight: Deploying specialized, smaller models (micro-models) for distinct tasks (e.g., bot detection, sentiment analysis) reduces reliance on large, expensive LLMs and allows for targeted scaling.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Train or fine-tune lightweight models for each detection task. Route requests to the smallest model that can handle the job, only escalating to larger models when necessary.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your data volume is low or your use case requires deep context understanding that only large models provide.

takeaways.title: Parallelize Real-Time Data Ingestion
takeaways.insight: Ariadne’s support for multiple social platforms implies parallel data connectors. Parallel ingestion pipelines can dramatically reduce latency and increase throughput for real-time analytics.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Implement separate ingestion workers for each platform/source. Use async processing or multi-threading to fetch and process data concurrently.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your sources have strict rate limits or if data volume is low enough that serial processing suffices.
takeaways.example:
# Async ingestion (Python pseudo-code)
async def fetch_all():
    await asyncio.gather(fetch_twitter(), fetch_reddit(), fetch_telegram())

takeaways.title: Automate Instant Report Generation
takeaways.insight: Instant, AI-generated reports and briefings reduce manual work for users. Automating this via templated LLM prompts or code enables faster time-to-value and higher user satisfaction.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Create reusable prompt templates or report generation scripts that pull from your analytics pipeline and format outputs for end users.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your reports require extensive human curation or if data is too sensitive to automate.
takeaways.example:
# Templated prompt (YAML)
prompt: |
  Generate a summary report of narrative trends and bot activity for the past 24 hours:
  - Key narratives
  - Detected bots
  - Recommended actions

takeaways.title: Build Vertical Data Moats via Custom Connectors
takeaways.insight: Valent’s ability to ingest from fragmented or restricted platforms (e.g., Telegram, Bluesky) creates a proprietary data advantage. Building and maintaining custom scrapers or leveraging partnerships can provide unique datasets competitors lack.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Identify data sources critical to your domain. Build custom connectors or scrapers, respecting legal and ethical boundaries, to access and normalize this data into your pipeline.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If platform TOS prohibit scraping or if the data is not materially valuable for your use case.
takeaways.example:
# Custom connector skeleton (Python pseudo-code)
def fetch_telegram(channel):
    # Use Telethon or similar library
    ...
    return messages

quick_wins: Parallelize your data ingestion pipelines using async workers for each source.
Automate basic report generation with templated prompts and LLMs.
Start modularizing detection tasks into separate services or agents.
deeper_dives: Research agentic AI architectures for scalable, modular analytics.
Explore micro-model mesh strategies for balancing cost and accuracy.
Investigate legal/ethical considerations for custom data connectors and scrapers.

headlines.headline: Why Valent Projects is betting AGAINST one-size-fits-all AI dashboards for disinfo detection
headlines.subheadline: Valent's agentic architecture challenges the generic social listening model, but is modularity enough to stand out?

headlines.headline: I reverse-engineered Valent's Ariadne platform—here's what 'multiagent AI for comms teams' really means
headlines.subheadline: Digging into Valent's tech reveals how their agent-based approach differs from typical sentiment trackers.

headlines.headline: Valent Projects is using Palantir’s playbook—vertical data moats for narrative warfare
headlines.subheadline: Like Palantir, Valent is betting on proprietary data and modular AI agents to lock in clients on reputation and bot detection.

headlines.headline: The modular agent trick every AI comms team should steal from Valent Projects
headlines.subheadline: Valent’s multiagent architecture could let teams customize narrative tracking and predictive response, if it’s more than vaporware.

headlines.headline: Is Valent Projects just another dashboard riding the AI hype cycle? Let’s find out.
headlines.subheadline: Their buzzword-heavy pitch raises the question: is there real IP or just clever marketing?

best_headline.headline: Is Valent Projects just another dashboard riding the AI hype cycle? Let’s find out.
best_headline.reason: This headline is direct, provocative, and speaks to the skepticism of technical readers. It promises a real investigation into substance vs. hype, which is exactly what engineers and founders want.

viral_hooks.social_media_version: Is Valent Projects just another dashboard riding the AI hype cycle? Let’s find out.

hook.opening_line: Every election, crisis, and viral moment is shaped by unseen forces—who's really pulling the strings online?
hook.stakes: The battle against digital manipulation is escalating, with reputations, elections, and social stability on the line.
hook.why_now: With platform fragmentation and AI-driven misinformation surging, the need for real-time, cross-platform intelligence has never been more urgent.

discovery.surface_story: Valent Projects claims its Ariadne platform uses multiagent AI to detect narratives, bots, and sentiment shifts across every major social channel.
discovery.deeper_story: Under the hood, Ariadne's modular, agent-based approach is rare in social listening, integrating detection, prediction, and scenario testing into a unified workflow—suggesting serious engineering to handle fragmented APIs and real-time analytics.
discovery.surprise: Despite the technical ambition, Valent's public pitch leans heavily on buzzwords and case studies, with little evidence of proprietary IP or defensible data advantage.

analysis.technical_insight: Ariadne's architecture hints at async data ingestion, micro-model meshes, and continuous-learning flywheels—an advanced but challenging stack for real-time, multi-platform analysis.
analysis.competitive_insight: Valent sits between legacy dashboards and next-gen AI comms tools, but without clear IP or exclusive data, its moat is only medium.
analysis.tension: Bull case: Valent cracks cross-platform, real-time narrative detection with agentic AI and becomes indispensable for crisis teams. Bear case: It's just another dashboard, easily outpaced by deeper-pocketed incumbents or open-source upstarts.

resolution.takeaway: Modularity and agentic architectures are the future of social intelligence, but defensibility will hinge on unique data and real technical depth.
resolution.prediction: Valent will need to reveal genuine IP or data partnerships to avoid being swept aside as AI commoditizes social listening.
resolution.call_to_action: Builders: Start modularizing your detection stack and parallelizing ingestion; analysts: watch for real technical disclosures, not just buzzwords.

suggested_visuals: Comparison chart: agentic vs legacy architectures
Infographic of social platform fragmentation and data connectors

unique_voice_content:
```markdown
### Valent Projects: Disinfo Detectives, or Just Another AI Dashboard?

Ever wish your comms team had a SWAT unit for online disinformation? Valent Projects is betting you do, pitching an "agentic AI" platform that’s supposed to sniff out narrative manipulation, bots, and sketchy trends across the internet. Sounds like an absolute godsend—if you believe the marketing.

#### The Core Insight

Here’s where it gets interesting: Valent isn’t just slapping sentiment analysis onto Twitter feeds. Their Ariadne platform claims to orchestrate a swarm of AI agents that not only flag the usual bots and astroturf campaigns, but also predict how narratives might evolve—like running a “what if” scenario tree for viral garbage. It’s a clever riff on multiagent architectures you’d see in robotics or trading bots, but pointed at comms and reputation management. If they pull it off, comms teams get more than dashboards—they get a sort of digital wargaming room for internet drama.

#### The Technical Meat

Let’s peel back the curtain. The promise: real-time ingestion from the Wild West of social platforms (including Telegram and Bluesky, not just Twitter and Reddit), entity resolution across fragmented data, and an “assistant agent” that spits out insights or reports on demand. This is non-trivial engineering—especially given the API lockdown arms race and the fun of parsing Telegram group chats without getting banned. If they truly have robust connectors and can resolve entities across platforms, that’s a technical feat, especially at scale.

But here’s the rub: for all the multiagent and RAG bravado, there’s a glaring lack of technical receipts. No evidence of custom models, no hints of proprietary data, not even a whiff of a unique retrieval pipeline. Everything described could, in theory, be duct-taped together with off-the-shelf LLMs, basic social listening APIs, and a decent UX layer. The “agentic” bit feels more like a branding exercise than a breakthrough—at least, until they show some real work under the hood.

Automated reports and context-aware chatbots? Useful, yes, but hardly defensible moats in 2024. Unless Valent is holding back some serious IP or unique data assets, they’re at risk of being just another AI-powered dashboard with a slick pitch deck.

#### The Honest Assessment

Would I use this? If I were running crisis comms for a big brand, maybe—as long as the real-time connectors held up and the UX didn’t suck. But as a builder, I’d want to see what’s actually proprietary here before betting on their stack. If you’re a comms pro facing daily troll armies, Ariadne might save you some headaches. For devs or AI folks looking for technical inspiration? Meh—unless Valent starts open-sourcing or publishing real technical details, there isn’t much to steal here.

#### The Builder Takeaway

- **Don’t confuse multiagent buzzwords with actual AI differentiation.** If you’re pitching “agentic” architectures, show your technical cards.
- **Integration is hard—especially across hostile, shifting APIs.** If you can build robust connectors and cross-platform entity resolution, that’s a real (if fragile) edge.
- **Reporting and RAG-powered chatbots are table stakes now.** If that’s your feature set, look for a deeper moat—unique data, proprietary models, or network effects.
- **Your moat isn’t your UX—it’s your data or your algorithms.** If your secret sauce is just a fancy workflow, competitors will catch up fast.
- **Transparency builds trust.** If you want to win over technical buyers, open up about your stack and what’s really novel.

#### The Prediction

Unless Valent gets serious about technical transparency or ships something genuinely proprietary, they’ll be another dashboard vendor fighting for attention in a crowded, noisy market. Prove me wrong, Valent: show us the IP, or expect to be copied by every agency with a Zapier account.
```
